name: East Flanders (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the natural environment of the province
the province's four major rivers (Scheldt, Lys, Dender and Durme)

description: Three horizontal bands in proportions 5:6:5: the top and bottom bands solid green, and the wider middle band divided lengthwise into seven segments in proportions 1:1:1:1:4:1:1 alternating white and green. In the fly, charged across the bands, a black lion rampant with red tongue and claws.
history: Imposed on 14 December 1998 by decree of the Flemish Minister of Culture, Family and Welfare, published in the Belgian official gazette on 14 April 1999. The green field references the province's environmental character, the white segments represent its four major rivers, and the black lion ties the province to the historic County of Flanders.

features.symbolism: three horizontal bands of green and white, with the white middle band carrying alternating green segments representing the province's rivers

features.role: Flemish lion (Vlaamse Leeuw), rampant lion with red tongue and claws, in the fly across the three bands
features.symbolism: the historic lion of the County of Flanders
